characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: AnOmnidisciplinary Scientistwho leads the $50 Billion time travel experiment, Project Quantum Leap. Theorizing that one could time travel within his own lifetime, he invents and tests atime machinein 1995, then vanishes. Trapped in the past, Beckett finds himself leaping from life to life, striving to put right what once went wrong, and hoping each time that his next leap, will be the leap back to his own body at PQL, in the 1990s. Absent-Minded Professor: He wasn't this before he started leaping, but it happens thanks to the "Swiss cheese" effect on his memory. Academic Athlete: He played basketball at varsity level in high school and was hoping for a basketball scholarship at Indiana State, but his brother talks him out of it because his real talent lay in quantum physics. The Ace: Sam has six different doctorates, is a Nobel laureate, is a classically trained pianist, sings tenor, is skilled in a number of martial arts, and is fluent in several languages. However, his Swiss cheese memory meanshe only seems to remember them when it's relevant to the plot. All-Loving Hero:There are definitely people that Sam is not fond of, but by and large (especially for the era the show was produced in), Sam is an incredibly empathetic and open person who truly wants to help everyone, even those that society has deemed unworthy of happiness and respect.

characters.name: Sam Beckett
characters.description: AnOmnidisciplinary Scientistwho leads the $50 Billion time travel experiment, Project Quantum Leap. Theorizing that one could time travel within his own lifetime, he invents and tests atime machinein 1995, then vanishes. Trapped in the past, Beckett finds himself leaping from life to life, striving to put right what once went wrong, and hoping each time that his next leap, will be the leap back to his own body at PQL, in the 1990s. Absent-Minded Professor: He wasn't this before he started leaping, but it happens thanks to the "Swiss cheese" effect on his memory. Academic Athlete: He played basketball at varsity level in high school and was hoping for a basketball scholarship at Indiana State, but his brother talks him out of it because his real talent lay in quantum physics. The Ace: Sam has six different doctorates, is a Nobel laureate, is a classically trained pianist, sings tenor, is skilled in a number of martial arts, and is fluent in several languages. However, his Swiss cheese memory meanshe only seems to remember them when it's relevant to the plot. All-Loving Hero:There are definitely people that Sam is not fond of, but by and large (especially for the era the show was produced in), Sam is an incredibly empathetic and open person who truly wants to help everyone, even those that society has deemed unworthy of happiness and respect.

characters.name: Al Calavicci
characters.description: The project observer at Quantum Leap. Al appears to Samuel Beckett as a neurological hologram broadcast from the future that only Sam can see and hear (usually), and gives him future information in order to help his leaps. 20 Minutes into the Future: Where Al lived, in relation to the series' original airdates. Eventually, the series set the year to 1999. The Ace: Not quite to the extent as Sam, but Al is incredibly capable in his own right. Anything Sam isn't that good at, Al usually does have the relevant skill and is able to help. For example, Al has acted as an Italian and Hungarian interpreter. He also coached Sam in dancing at a Jewish party. Ace Pilot: He started out as a naval aviator, gaining combat experience in Vietnam, where he wound up being taken prisoner. The skill comes into play in "Genesis", where he assists Sam in piloting a test plane. Berserk Button: Al, despite his womanizing ways, reacts quite badly to the mistreatment of women. He also detests ill treatment of the mentally handicapped, and is very critical of mothers who abandon their children. This can be attributed to his upbringing. He's also critical of anyone who does anything to harm the environment.

characters.name: Ziggy
characters.description: Ziggy is the supercomputer and A.I. in charge of Project Quantum Leap. She assists Al with providing future information to Sam via Al's handlink. Alternate Self: In the timeline where Al died in the gas chamber in 1957, Ziggy is known as "Alpha." Ambiguous Gender: Al and Sam refer to Ziggy with male pronouns early on, but start calling it "she" (with some exceptions) after her feminine voice is heard. Benevolent A.I.: Despite having occasional temper tantrums andBarbra Streisand's ego, Ziggy happily enjoys assisting Sam and Al. Brain in a Jar: The script for "The Leap Back" describes Ziggy's glowing blue orb as being "brain tissue in a nutrient bath."

characters.name: Gushie
characters.description: Gushie is the lead computer programmer at Project Quantum Leap. He assists Al in the Imaging Chamber via Ziggy's main terminal, and can serve as a backup hologram in case of an emergency. Absent-Minded Professor: When he meets Leon Stiles, who is in the process of escaping from the Waiting Room, he casually greets him as "Dr. Beckett." You know, despite the fact that he should be well aware of leapees after helping the rest of the project assist Sam forfour years. Butt-Monkey: A mild example. Gushie's bad breath makes him the butt of jokes (it's the first thing that Sam's Swiss-cheesed mind remembers about Gushie), and in "Killin' Time," Leon Stiles steals his jacket and his car. Despite this, Gushie is very good-natured and capable, and gets along with pretty much everyone. Fanon: His real name isn't revealed in the series, but a non-canon novel once named him "Dr. Irving Gushman" and it stuck in the fandom's minds. Genius Ditz: Gushie calls a leapee "Dr. Beckett" after four years of Sam being gone, and when Sam eventually full-body leaps, Gushie helps Al scan Sam's birthdays but doesn't think to include the actual day of Sam's birth. However, Gushie can also be very clever and focused, managing to jury rig the Imaging Chamber on the fly in order to speak to Sam (in the past) and Al (in the present) during the Leon Stiles situation.

characters.name: Dr. Beeks
characters.description: Dr. Beeks is the Project's head psychiatrist. She greets the leapees in the Waiting Room, calms them down after the shock of leaping, and uses their knowledge to build a profile for Sam to use in the past. Out of Focus: Beeks gets mentioned a whole lot, but she only appears on screen in "Shock Theater" and "The Leap Back." It's jarring in episodes like "Return of the Evil Leaper," where she is talked about, and leapee Arnold Watkins mistakes Al's handlink for her, but it'sAlwho does some armchair psychology with Arnold, not Beeks. Twofer Token Minority: She is African-American and female. The Voiceless: Appears in "Shock Theater" as a hologram alongside Al, but Sam can't hear her speak due to the Imaging Chamber's power limitations. When she makes her only other appearance an episode later, she has no lines.

characters.name: Donna Eleese
characters.description: Donna is Sam Beckett's first love, who left him at the altar in the original timeline. After reuniting her with her father in 1972, the timeline changed so that she had been married to Sam, and her presence with the Project ensured that an oversight committee was not needed. Freudian Excuse: In the original timeline, she jilts one fiance, then later Sam, at the altar due to intimacy issues stemming from not knowing her father. During Sam's third leap, he gets Donna to meet her father, undoing the Freudian Excuse and ending up married to her back in the future. I Will Wait for You: Donna realizes that Sam's quantum leaping his helping the world, and permits him to continue, telling Al that Sam can never know about her until he returns. Ultimately becomes a tragic example when Samdoesn'treturn. Runaway Bride: In the original timeline, Donna did this twice over, with Sam as the second jilted groom. Sam resolving herFreudian Excuseduring his third leap alters the timeline so that this doesn't occur. Sadistic Choice: Donna has to choose whether to send Sam back to 1945 to save Al (which could overwhelm the retrieval program since it's outside Sam's lifetime), or keep Sam home in 1999 and let Al die. She chooses to activate the accelerator and is forced to watch the retrieval program fail.

characters.name: Tina Martinez
characters.description: Tina is a technician at the project, and Al's girlfriend. Buxom Beauty Standard: One of the things Al likes about his girlfriend Tina is her large breasts, which he admits in "Dr. Ruth", though he awkwardly can't bring himself to say the word "breasts" and uses aHurricane of Euphemismsinstead, like casabas, melons, ho-has, honkers, hooters, headlights, ta-tas, teeters, tweeters, tom-toms, tee-tees. Child Prodigy: Tina was a child prodigy, graduating from high school when she was13and ha her college degree at16. The Other Darrin: Tina first appeared in the pilot, played byAmanda Horan Kennedy, and credited as "Barbra Horan". In the pilot, Tina was a tall, confident, deep-voiced woman with a sultry sense of fashion. Also, the pilot implies that this was the first time Al and Tina met, as they never call each other by their names, he tries to distract her from the colored light eminating from the mountain, and she appears confused when Al talks to Gooshie. When recast withGigi Ricein Season 4, Tina appears instead as a ditzy, high-voiced woman with a teenager's sense of fashion, and is a full-time member of PQLAnd she's having an affair with Gooshie. Satellite Love Interest: Her role in the show mostly revolves around being Al's sexy girlfriend. In multiple episodes where she doesn't make an appearance, Al talks about their up-and-down relationship.

characters.name: Sammie Jo Fuller
characters.description: Sam's biological daughter fathered with Abigail Fuller during the "Trilogy" three-parter. Like her father, she has a high IQ and eidetic memory, becomes a member of the project after Sam alters time, and is working on a theory to bring Sam home. The Ghost: We don't see Sammie Jo's future self who works at PQL. Hero of Another Story: From Al's description, her future self working at PQL is utterly brilliant, and is in the process of developing some promising retrieval theories behind the scenes. Had the show continued for another season, she likely would have become a leaper herself to save Sam. Had theSci-Fi ChannelSpin-Offseen the light of day, Sammie Jo would have been the protagonist. Identical Grandson: She looks exactly like her mother Abigail did at the same age, and shares the same actress. Kid from the Future: A variation, in that she's an adult working at PQL in 1999, but was born in 1967 and technically is only 14 years younger than her biological father.

characters.name: Alia
characters.description: Alia is a quantum leaper from an unknown leaping project. Unlike Sam, she is likely a test subject and doesn't seem to be a person in charge, and her project's goals are malevolent. The Atoner: Helps Sam complete his missions in "Return" and "Revenge." Because You Were Nice to Me: During their first meeting, Sam and Alia have a long heart-to-heart about quantum leaping and find some common ground, which later on allows him to talk her down when she tries to murder him on Lothos' orders. The second time Alia crosses paths with Sam, she's visibly distraught at the thought of harming him, and eventually joins up with him because he's much kinder thanthe alternative. Bilingual Bonus:Aliain Latin is the feminine form of the adjectivealiusmeaning "another" or "other", befitting another leaper who happens to be a woman. Body Horror:Somethingunpleasant happens to Aliawhen she leaps outat the end of "Deliver Us from Evil," but it's unclear exactlywhat. Upon failing to kill Sam, Alia's image gets distorted and she screams in pain, then there is a flash as she leaps out and theReset Buttonis pressed (thereby undoing her damage to theLaMottafamily). The next time the leapers meet, Alia appears physically unscathed, but she tells Sam that Lothos put her through some unspeakable torture.

characters.name: Zoey
characters.description: Zoey is Alia's holographic contact from the evil leaping project. She seems to be in a position of authority in the project. She later becomes a leaper herself when Alia dual-leaps with Sam. All Women Are Lustful: She's a horndog and absolutely relishes it. Big Bad: For the "Evil Leaper" trilogy. She's not the head of her project but she's the most active antagonist, especially when she leaps in herself to try and murder Alia. Deadpan Snarker: Constantly snarks at people in Alia's leaps. Devil in Plain Sight: When she leaps into Clifton Meyers, she resumes her usual evil behavior, which is easily masked by the fact that her leapee is already a bit of a jerkass.

characters.name: Lothos
characters.description: Lothos is the computer in charge of the evil leaping project. He appears to have some degree of control over Alia and Zoey's leaps, and sets up Alia's missions in the past. A.I. Is a Crapshoot: Lothos is evil for some unknown reason, sending Alia and Zoey on missions to ruin history, and it is mentioned that he has tortured Alia in the past. Bad Boss: Lothos tortures Alia for failing to drive theLaMottafamily apart and kill Sam; this is largely what motivates Alia to switch allegiances the next time she runs into Sam. Cold-Blooded Torture: Does this to Alia after failing to defeat Sam the first time, an experience she describes as "worse than death." It eventually leads her to defect. Enemy-Detecting Radar: Lothos can detect the presence of other leapers if he's actively looking for them (such as when Zoey does a quick scan after hearing "Arnold" say Al's name), but he cannot pinpoint whom they leaped into.

characters.name: Thames
characters.description: Thames is a secondary observer from the evil project. He becomes Zoey's observer when she leaps into the past to hunt Alia. Affably Evil: Thames seems like a pleasant, funny guy who might be a lot of fun to be around, but he openly endorses killing Alia and Sam, and seems delighted by the idea. Classy Cane: Carries one in his right hand, and holds his handlink in the other hand. Evil Counterpart:Probablymeant as one for Gushie, in that he works behind the scenes on the evil project, and can be a backup hologram when needed. He also serves as something of aDistaff Counterpartto Gushie, who is a twitchy nebbish, whereas Thames is loud and obnoxious. Evil Is Hammy: He only gets really hammy when he's gushing over how fun it is to be a hologram. When he calms down, he becomes a lot more subtle.

characters.name: Beth Calavicci
characters.description: Beth Calavicci is Al's first wife, and the only woman Al has ever really loved. Beth was a US Navy nursing officer while Al was a pilot. On Al's second tour of duty in Vietnam, he was shot down and taken captive; Beth had no way of knowing if he was still alive and she remarried. Al never got over her. Absence Makes the Heart Go Yonder: In the original timeline, Beth loses hope that Al is alive while MIA in Vietnam, and she marries Dirk Simon after Dirk convinces her to have Al declaredLegally Dead. At the very end of the series, however, Sam returns to 1969 to reassure Beth that Al is still alive, thus saving Al's marriage. Ascended Extra: In the original series, Beth made only two appearances as aSmall Role, Big Impactcharacter for Al. Inthe sequel series, Beth comes back and plays a slightly bigger role than before as a more recurring supporting character with five total episodic appearances. Back for the Finale: In the series finale, Sam returns to April 3, 1969 during the events of "M.I.A." and tells Beth about Al to save their marriage. Happily Married: Whilst only together for two years. Al looks back at their time together with fondness. Sam fixing the timeline for Al means she and Al stay together, have four daughters, and celebrate their 39th wedding anniversary in June 2000.
